message:
docs: update CI pipeline diagram to 8 jobs with compiler-matrix lane; simplify adaptive hedge counter wrapping

- Expand README CI diagram from 7 to 8 top-level jobs, adding the
  compiler-matrix lane (GCC/Clang x 4 profiles) and updating the check
  lane description to include docs/browser/MC steps
- Simplify adaptive_hedge_record counter: remove UINT32_MAX guard and
  allow natural wrapping with a comment explaining the brief
  recalibration tradeoff (10 records to refill the window)
- Sync beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-ekb3","title":"EPIC: Hardcoded Constants, Stale Comments, and Code Quality Remediation","description":"Master epic for systematically addressing all hardcoded magic numbers that should be configurable,
stale/misleading comments indicating unfinished code, and related code quality issues discovered
during a comprehensive fresh-eyes audit of the entire asupersync ANSI C codebase.

## Background

A full-project audit was conducted scanning every .c and .h file for:
1. Hardcoded numeric constants in function bodies that should be named #define macros
2. Comments containing "will", "would", "TODO", "FIXME" indicating unfinished or deferred work
3. Stale documentation that no longer matches the actual implementation state

## Scope

This epic covers:
- Converting 4 hardcoded magic numbers to named, #ifndef-guarded compile-time constants
- Updating 2 stale header comments that incorrectly describe code as stubs/future work
- Identifying and documenting items verified as correct (not needing changes)
- Ensuring all changes compile cleanly with -Werror and pass existing test suites

## Architectural Principle

The asx project uses a "resource contracts instead of silent degradation" philosophy (README).
Every operational parameter should be:
1. Named (self-documenting in code)
2. Overridable at compile time (#ifndef guard)
3. Documented with its default value and unit
4. Consistent with the profile/resource-class system

This ensures embedded deployers (R1/R2/R3 resource classes) and specialized profiles
(HFT, AUTOMOTIVE) can tune thresholds without modifying source code.

## Verification

All changes MUST:
- Build cleanly with `make build` (strict -Werror)
- Pass all existing unit tests
- Not change any runtime behavior when using default values
- Be backward-compatible (existing code that doesn't override the macros gets identical behavior)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:02:45.202825037Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:16:14.362635656Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:16:14.362076177Z","close_reason":"All tasks completed: 4 constants extracted, 2 comments fixed, audit trail documented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","code-quality","constants"]}
 {"id":"bd-ekb3.1","title":"SUB-EPIC: Hardcoded Magic Numbers → Named Compile-Time Constants","description":"Convert all hardcoded magic numbers in function bodies to named #define macros with
#ifndef guards, enabling compile-time override by deployers.

## Why This Matters

The asx project targets 9 deployment profiles (CORE through BROWSER) and 3 resource
classes (R1/R2/R3). Each profile has different operational envelopes. Hardcoded constants
prevent deployers from tuning behavior without source modification, violating the
"resource contracts instead of silent degradation" principle.

## Pattern

For each constant:
1. Add `#ifndef ASX_<MODULE>_<NAME>` / `#define` / `#endif` in the relevant public header
2. Replace the literal in the .c file with the macro name
3. Update any doc comments referencing the old literal value
4. Verify the default value matches the original literal exactly (zero behavior change)

## Constants Identified

| Constant | File | Value | Unit | New Macro |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Process wait poll guard | process.c:235 | 10000 | polls | ASX_PROCESS_WAIT_MAX_POLLS |
| App default poll budget | app.c:125 | 10000 | polls | ASX_APP_DEFAULT_POLL_BUDGET |
| HFT p99 gate threshold | hft_instrument.c:281 | 10000 | nanoseconds | ASX_HFT_DEFAULT_P99_NS |
| Automotive watchdog period | automotive_instrument.c:250,258 | 10000000 | nanoseconds | ASX_AUTO_DEFAULT_WATCHDOG_PERIOD_NS |

## Items Audited and Verified as Correct (No Change Needed)

These were examined and confirmed to be intentionally placed:
- hooks.c:62 `return 0` — Intentional platform hook stub; overridden at runtime init
- lab.c:57,93 `1000000ULL` tick_ns — Default in config struct; already user-configurable via asx_lab_config
- console.c:84 "would check isatty()" — Design note explaining platform-specific behavior
- plan.c:413 "this would set up a deadline" — Explaining deterministic simulation vs real async
- Profile overload thresholds (90/85/80/75%) in overload_catalog.c — Intentional per-profile policy
- Seqlock spike retry limits (100, 1000) — Experimental research code, not production path","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:01.938649835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:16:12.135003483Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:16:12.134303087Z","close_reason":"All 4 hardcoded constants extracted to named #ifndef-guarded macros","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["code-quality","constants","hardcoded"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ekb3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ekb3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:01.938649835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -1604,23 +1604,27 @@ The `docs/` directory contains 60+ files covering architecture, decisions, verif
 
 ## CI Pipeline Architecture
 
-The CI pipeline runs 7 parallel lanes on every push and PR:
+The primary CI workflow (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) currently runs 8 top-level
+jobs on pushes and PRs:
 
 ```
 ┌────────────┐┌────────────────┐┌────────────────┐┌────────────────┐
 │   check    ││ unit-invariant ││  conformance   ││ profile-parity │
-│  format    ││  148 unit      ││  Rust fixture  ││  cross-profile │
-│  8 lints   ││  3 invariant   ││  parity        ││  digest match  │
-│  build     ││                ││                ││                │
+│ format +   ││  148 unit      ││  Rust fixture  ││  cross-profile │
+│ lint/docs/ ││  3 invariant   ││  parity        ││  digest match  │
+│ browser/MC ││  vignettes     ││  codec equiv   ││                │
 └────────────┘└────────────────┘└────────────────┘└────────────────┘
-┌──────────────┐┌──────────────────┐┌──────────────┐
-│  fuzz-parity ││  embedded-matrix ││     e2e      │
-│  diff fuzz   ││  mipsel/armv7/   ││  17 scen-    │
-│  minimize    ││  aarch64/RISC-V  ││  arios       │
-└──────────────┘└──────────────────┘└──────────────┘
+┌──────────────┐┌──────────────────┐┌──────────────┐┌────────────────┐
+│  fuzz-parity ││  embedded-matrix ││     e2e      ││compiler-matrix │
+│  diff fuzz   ││  mipsel/armv7/   ││  17 scen-    ││ GCC/Clang x    │
+│  minimize    ││  aarch64/RISC-V  ││  arios       ││ 4 profiles     │
+└──────────────┘└──────────────────┘└──────────────┘└────────────────┘
 ```
 
-All 7 lanes must pass before merge. A nightly workflow extends fuzz runs to 100K iterations and collects performance baselines. The release workflow is tag-triggered (`v*`) and produces signed artifact bundles with SHA-256 checksums and Sigstore signatures.
+All 8 jobs must pass before merge. A nightly workflow extends fuzz runs to
+100K iterations and collects performance baselines. The release workflow is
+tag-triggered (`v*`) and produces signed artifact bundles with SHA-256
+checksums and Sigstore signatures.
 
 ## Troubleshooting
